title: digitaria timorensis subsp. blepharophora
intro: Digitaria timorensis subsp. blepharophora, also known as East Timor finger grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties, making it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Digitaria timorensis subsp. blepharophora include its potential to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing and skin infections. It is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for herbal use, and can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
